text:
A Startup's Guide to Technical Screening

Building the perfect team is vital for startup growth.

What is Technical Screening? Technical screening is an evaluation strategy assessing multiple candidates' technical aptitude. These customized evaluations verify whether applicants possess required skills for specific positions.

5 Ways Technical Screening Benefits Startups:
1. Reliable talent acquisitions: Micro-analyzing applicants for core concepts ensures employers identify candidates who can perform job requirements effectively.
2. Reduced hiring times and costs: Technical screening narrows viable candidate pools. Job seekers spend '11 hours a week on average looking for work,' making faster hiring critical.
3. Tech screening eliminates unconscious bias: Assessment-based evaluation removes human bias, allowing purely merit-based candidate evaluation.
4. Employee retention and reduced turnover: Replacing entry-level employees costs '30-50% of the annual salary,' while senior roles cost up to '400% of the annual salary.'
5. Data-driven hiring decisions: Objective, merit-based screening validates hiring decisions through measurable performance metrics.

How to Conduct Effective Technical Screening:
- Identify potential talent sources: internally (previous applicants, employee referrals) or externally (LinkedIn, social media). Glassdoor reports '79% of job seekers claim they are likely to search for jobs on social media platforms.'
- Pick the right screening tool: Select platforms offering specific skill vetting, comprehensive question repositories, automated reports.
- Invite applicants: Use automation features to invite large talent pools efficiently.
- Filter best candidates: Review automated scores across multiple performance metrics.
- Conduct virtual interviews: Remote interviews overcome geographical constraints.
- Make confident offers: Armed with data-driven insights, extend offers to best-qualified candidates.

The article recommends WeCP as a comprehensive technical screening platform.
